page: Amazon Web Services m6idn.24xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the Intel Xeon 8375C processor. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this server provides 96 virtual CPUs across 48 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. It features a dedicated CPU allocation running at a clock speed of 3.5 GHz, supported by a 54 MB L3 cache. The hardware configuration includes 384.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, translating to 4.0 GB of RAM per core.

The instance is built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and features 5700 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. It provides a baseline network bandwidth of 150 Gbps, making it highly optimized for network-intensive and Elastic Block Store workloads. No dedicated GPUs are included in this configuration.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core performance, with stress-ng multi-core and Passmark CPU scores ranking in the top 25%. The hardware demonstrates top-tier cryptographic capabilities, placing in the top 10% for the Passmark CPU encryption test. Memory bandwidth is also highly efficient, reaching 328.42 GB/sec for large block reads. However, single-core performance and large-model LLM text generation remain average to weak. This profile makes the instance highly suitable for database operations, high-throughput web serving, and memory-intensive general-purpose applications.
description: The Amazon Web Services m6idn.24xlarge is a general-purpose, network-optimized instance featuring 96 vCPUs and 384.0 GB of DDR4 memory. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz, it delivers strong multi-core performance and top-tier cryptographic execution, as evidenced by its top 10% Passmark encryption benchmark. The instance includes 5700 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 150 Gbps, managed via the Nitro hypervisor. While single-core speeds and large-scale LLM inference are moderate, its high memory bandwidth and massive local storage make it highly efficient for databases, static web serving, and high-throughput data processing.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m6idn.24xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 96 vCPUs, 384 GB DDR4 RAM, 5700 GB NVMe SSD storage, and 150 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering strong multi-core and cryptographic performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m6idn.24xlarge features 96 Intel vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, 5700 GB NVMe SSD, and 150 Gbps network bandwidth for high-throughput workloads.
tagline: A high-bandwidth general-purpose server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, massive local NVMe storage, and exceptional multi-core cryptographic performance.
bullet_points: 96 vCPUs and 384 GB DDR4 RAM powered by Intel Xeon 8375C processors.
5700 GB of local NVMe SSD storage for high-speed local data access.
150 Gbps baseline network bandwidth optimized for high-throughput workloads.
Top-tier cryptographic performance and strong multi-core benchmark results.
Well-suited for database operations, static web serving, and data processing.
